description: OpenAI OAuth provider defaults GPT-5.4 responses to low verbosity so routine replies stay concise.
steps: Run the OpenAI OAuth provider test and verify the request body contains text.verbosity set to low.
Confirm a simple system prompt like 'Be concise.' still passes through unchanged.
Verify the provider still returns parsed assistant text and tool calls after the payload change.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider regression tests reflect the current SSE-backed implementation instead of the removed AsyncOpenAI client path.
steps: Run the focused OpenAI OAuth test file.
Verify the test stubs the internal codex request helper rather than a removed SDK symbol.
Confirm the test fails if verbosity or request routing regresses.

description: Lazy provider exports include OpenAIOAuthProvider so package-level imports stay accurate.
steps: Run the provider init test file.
Verify nanobot.providers.__all__ includes OpenAIOAuthProvider.
Confirm importing OpenAIOAuthProvider through nanobot.providers works without eagerly importing unrelated backends.

description: Repository progress log records the current baseline issues discovered before the feature change.
steps: Open PROGRESS.md after the session update.
Verify it records the failing repo_sync baseline and stale provider test findings.
Confirm the next session can understand why only focused tests were used for this feature.

description: Harness init script can reproduce environment, smoke-test, and CLI-health checks without aborting on the first failure.
steps: Run ./init.sh manually.
Verify it exits successfully even if pytest reports failures.
Confirm it prints where failing logs were written.

description: Document the exact behavioral difference between nanobot and openclaw OpenAI reply verbosity defaults.
steps: Compare the nanobot and openclaw provider payload builders.
Verify the recorded difference mentions medium versus low verbosity.
Confirm the comparison references both code paths accurately.

description: OpenAI Codex provider parity is evaluated so nanobot can later align concise behavior across OAuth-backed OpenAI transports.
steps: Inspect the OpenAI Codex payload builder.
Verify whether it still defaults text.verbosity to medium.
Record parity follow-up notes without changing behavior unexpectedly.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider keeps tool calling enabled after the concise default change.
steps: Run the focused OpenAI OAuth provider tests.
Verify the request still includes converted function tools and auto tool_choice.
Confirm tool call parsing still returns the expected call id and arguments.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider keeps reasoning payload support after the concise default change.
steps: Run the OpenAI OAuth provider tests with reasoning_effort configured.
Verify the request body still includes reasoning.effort when provided.
Confirm there is no regression to finish_reason handling.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still respects explicit model prefixes for hyphen and underscore variants.
steps: Run the strip_model_prefix unit tests.
Verify both openai-oauth/gpt-5.4 and openai_oauth/gpt-5.4 resolve correctly.
Confirm gpt-5.4-mini remains supported.

description: Provider retry behavior remains unaffected by the concise OpenAI OAuth payload change.
steps: Run provider retry tests relevant to generation defaults if touched later.
Verify generation defaults still flow through chat_with_retry.
Confirm no retry-only regressions are introduced by the feature.

description: Agent defaults continue to pass max_tokens, temperature, and reasoning_effort into provider generation settings.
steps: Inspect CLI provider construction after the change.
Verify no existing generation wiring is removed.
Confirm the concise behavior comes from payload verbosity rather than breaking generation defaults.

description: Session notes clearly distinguish baseline red tests from regressions introduced by the concise-response feature.
steps: Review the latest PROGRESS.md entry.
Verify it names the unrelated repo_sync failure separately from feature verification.
Confirm the note explains why targeted tests are the reliable gate for this change.

description: Focused provider verification command is documented in progress notes for repeatable future sessions.
steps: Review PROGRESS.md after the session.
Verify the exact pytest command is recorded.
Confirm another session can rerun the same verification without rediscovery.

description: OpenAI OAuth errors remain user-readable after payload changes.
steps: Inspect the exception-to-LLMResponse path in the provider.
Verify the change does not alter the error wrapping branch.
Confirm the provider still returns finish_reason='error' on request failures.

description: Shared concise-verbosity helper feasibility is evaluated without coupling unrelated providers in this session.
steps: Review OpenAI OAuth and Codex payload builders side by side.
Verify whether a shared helper would be mechanical and low-risk.
Record the result in PROGRESS.md without forcing a broad refactor.

description: OpenAI OAuth default behavior stays compatible with gpt-5.4-mini as a lighter variant.
steps: Inspect the README and model prefix helper for the mini model path.
Verify no model-specific condition blocks low verbosity for gpt-5.4-mini.
Confirm tests still allow mini model prefix parsing.

description: OpenAI OAuth README guidance can later mention concise-mode expectations if user-facing docs need parity with behavior.
steps: Review the current OpenAI OAuth README section.
Verify whether it documents response-style behavior today.
Record whether a docs update is necessary after the code change.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider continues to use the ChatGPT backend codex responses endpoint with OAuth headers intact.
steps: Inspect the provider request path after the change.
Verify the endpoint and headers are unchanged.
Confirm only the verbosity default differs.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider test asserts the payload sent to _request_codex so future regressions are caught at the contract boundary.
steps: Run the focused provider tests.
Verify assertions cover model, instructions, text.verbosity, reasoning, tool_choice, and tools.
Confirm the test no longer depends on removed SDK internals.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still sets prompt cache keys after the concise change.
steps: Inspect the request body in the focused provider test.
Verify prompt_cache_key is still present.
Confirm it is derived from messages and not dropped during refactor.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still enables parallel tool calls after the concise change.
steps: Review the payload assertions in tests.
Verify parallel_tool_calls remains true.
Confirm no tool execution policy was changed incidentally.

description: Provider package lazy-import tests remain the single-source regression net for top-level export ordering.
steps: Run the provider init test file.
Verify export ordering matches current package expectations.
Confirm OpenAIOAuthProvider is represented in both __all__ and lazy imports.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still retries with verify=False only on certificate failures.
steps: Inspect the SSL fallback branch after the concise change.
Verify the exception filter remains limited to certificate failures.
Confirm the new behavior does not broaden retry scope.

description: Targeted verification stays fast enough for per-feature checkpoints in this repo.
steps: Run the focused pytest subset for this feature.
Verify it completes successfully without invoking the unrelated failing baseline test module.
Confirm the command is practical for future feature-level checkpoints.

description: Progress log records that openclaw achieves shorter replies via low verbosity rather than a different GPT-5.4 version.
steps: Read the comparison notes in PROGRESS.md.
Verify they mention strategy difference rather than model-version difference.
Confirm a later session can reuse the comparison without re-reading openclaw.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider preserves user system prompts instead of replacing them with a fixed concise prompt.
steps: Run the focused provider tests.
Verify the request instructions equal the caller-supplied system prompt.
Confirm concise behavior comes from text verbosity, not prompt rewriting.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider remains compatible with explicit provider forcing plus bare model names in config.
steps: Inspect README examples and prefix stripping behavior.
Verify provider forcing still resolves to gpt-5.4 correctly.
Confirm the concise default does not rely on the prefixed model string.

description: Regression tests continue to validate parsed tool call arguments as dictionaries after the request helper stub change.
steps: Run the focused OpenAI OAuth test.
Verify the response fixture exercises a function_call event.
Confirm the returned tool call arguments are parsed JSON objects.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still strips the provider prefix before sending the model to the backend.
steps: Run the strip_model_prefix test and focused provider test.
Verify the payload model equals gpt-5.4 rather than openai-oauth/gpt-5.4.
Confirm no backend request includes the prefixed model name.

description: Baseline unrelated failures are preserved as known blockers rather than silently masked by narrower tests.
steps: Review the current session notes.
Verify the missing nanobot.repo_sync.service failure is explicitly mentioned.
Confirm the session does not claim the whole repo is green.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider remains store-disabled after the concise-response change.
steps: Inspect the request body assertion in tests.
Verify store remains false.
Confirm the feature does not alter retention semantics.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider continues to stream responses while using low verbosity.
steps: Inspect the request body assertion in tests.
Verify stream remains true.
Confirm the concise change does not switch the provider to non-streaming mode.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider tests cover both content and finish_reason after the concise-response change.
steps: Run the focused provider tests.
Verify the response content assertion still passes.
Confirm the expected finish_reason remains tool_calls when tool calls are present.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider remains free of direct SDK coupling in the request path.
steps: Inspect the provider implementation.
Verify requests go through _request_codex rather than AsyncOpenAI.
Confirm tests align with the actual implementation.

description: The concise-response feature is verified with the repository-preferred .venv pytest interpreter.
steps: Run the focused pytest subset with .venv/bin/pytest.
Verify no system-python fallback is used.
Confirm the command succeeds after the code change.

description: Progress notes identify whether README behavior documentation should be updated in a future session.
steps: Inspect the final PROGRESS.md entry.
Verify it states whether the docs still omit verbosity behavior.
Confirm a future docs-only follow-up would be well-scoped.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider request payload continues to include reasoning.encrypted_content in the include list.
steps: Inspect the payload assertion in tests or code review notes.
Verify the include list remains unchanged.
Confirm only verbosity changed.

description: Focused concise-response change stays limited to OpenAI OAuth unless parity work is intentionally scheduled.
steps: Review the git diff after the feature is complete.
Verify no unrelated providers changed unless explicitly documented.
Confirm scope stayed within the agreed single feature.

description: Provider export tests remain isolated from unrelated repo baseline failures.
steps: Run tests/providers/test_providers_init.py directly.
Verify it passes independently.
Confirm success does not require the missing repo_sync module.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still builds OAuth headers from the stored token before sending requests.
steps: Review the focused provider test setup.
Verify the helper receives account_id and access token-derived headers.
Confirm the concise change does not disturb auth header wiring.

description: OpenAI OAuth config path differences between nanobot and openclaw are documented for future parity decisions.
steps: Review the comparison notes after the session.
Verify they distinguish nanobot's flat defaults from openclaw's model.primary plus model params.
Confirm the notes explain why openclaw can attach fastMode per model.

description: Low verbosity remains the default only when the provider builds the payload, not by mutating agent-wide prompt text.
steps: Inspect the final code diff.
Verify there is no global system prompt rewrite for concision.
Confirm the change is localized to request payload construction and tests.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider tests remain readable and maintainable after converting to request-helper stubs.
steps: Review the updated test file.
Verify the fixture setup is concise and follows current implementation boundaries.
Confirm future maintainers can see which payload fields define concise behavior.

description: Feature checkpoint commit message clearly describes the concise OpenAI OAuth behavior change.
steps: Inspect the feature commit message.
Verify it describes the behavior in a concise, specific way.
Confirm the required Codex co-author trailer is present exactly once.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still leaves tool_choice configurable by callers after the concise change.
steps: Inspect the focused provider test or final diff.
Verify tool_choice defaults to auto only when callers do not override it.
Confirm the concise feature does not hardcode a stricter tool policy.

description: OpenAI OAuth provider still uses the caller-supplied tools only when present.
steps: Inspect the provider implementation after the feature.
Verify the tools field is conditional and unchanged.
Confirm concise behavior does not alter tool schema conversion.

description: The harness plan can be resumed cleanly in a later session without redoing current diagnosis work.
steps: Review PLAN.json and PROGRESS.md together.
Verify the next session can see the completed concise-response feature and remaining follow-ups.
Confirm the state files match the current repository reality.

description: Future optional parity work for openai_codex concise defaults is explicitly separated from the current approved feature.
steps: Review the current session notes.
Verify codex parity is recorded as follow-up rather than silently bundled in.
Confirm the approved feature scope remains intact.

description: Session completion notes state exactly what was verified and what remains unverified due baseline failures.
steps: Read the final PROGRESS.md entry.
Verify it lists the passing focused tests and the unresolved repo baseline issue.
Confirm it does not overclaim full-repo validation.

description: Harness initialization checkpoint is committed so later sessions can restore task state before editing.
steps: Review git log after the init checkpoint.
Verify init.sh, PLAN.json, and PROGRESS.md are included in the commit.
Confirm the repository can resume from that checkpoint without reconstructing task context.

description: The current session ends with a clean checkpoint even if broader repo validation is still blocked by unrelated failures.
steps: Inspect git log and PROGRESS.md at session end.
Verify the feature work is committed.
Confirm the remaining blocker is documented for the next session.
